explanation: Upgradeable (proxy based) contracts cannot use a constructor, so they rely on an initializer function to set the owner and critical state. If that initializer is unprotected or is never called on the proxy, anyone can call it and claim ownership.
impact: An attacker front runs or simply calls the open initializer, becomes the owner or admin, and then upgrades the implementation or drains funds. Several major proxy hacks trace back to an uninitialized or re initializable proxy.
